sentences.sentence: The conviction that historical relics provide infallible testimony about the past is rooted in the nineteenth and early twentieth centuries, when science was regarded as objective and value free.
sentences.translation: 认为历史遗物能为过去提供绝对可靠证据的信念，根植于十九世纪和二十世纪初；当时，科学被视为客观且不受价值观影响。

structure.text: that historical relics provide infallible testimony about the past
is rooted in the nineteenth and early twentieth centuries
when science was regarded as objective and value free

difficult_points: 第一个 that 从句说明 conviction 的具体内容，是同位语从句。
when 从句修饰 centuries；was regarded as 是被动结构。

expressions.meaning_zh: 为……提供绝对可靠的证据

sentences.sentence: As one writer observes: 'Although it is now evident that artefacts are as easily altered as chronicles, public faith in their veracity endures: a tangible relic seems ipso facto real.'
sentences.translation: 正如一位作家所说：“尽管如今显然可见，文物和历史记载一样容易被篡改，公众对其真实性的信任却依然存在：有形的遗物似乎天然就是真实的。”

structure.text: Although it is now evident that artefacts are as easily altered as chronicles
that artefacts are as easily altered as chronicles
public faith in their veracity endures
a tangible relic seems ipso facto real

difficult_points: Although 从句中 it 是形式主语，that 从句是真正主语；as ... as ... 比较文物与记载被改动的容易程度。
冒号后的分句解释公众信任为何延续；ipso facto 意为“根据事实本身、自然就”。

sentences.sentence: Such conviction was, until recently, reflected in museum displays.
sentences.translation: 直到不久前，这种信念仍反映在博物馆的陈列方式中。
difficult_points: Such conviction 回指公众认为实物证据天然可靠的信念。
until recently 是插入的时间状语，was reflected in 表示信念体现在陈列中。

sentences.sentence: Museums used to look – and some still do – much like storage rooms of objects packed together in showcases: good for scholars who wanted to study the subtle differences in design, but not for the ordinary visitor, to whom it all looked alike.
sentences.translation: 博物馆过去看起来很像把物品密集塞进展柜的储藏室——有些现在仍是如此：这方便了想研究设计细微差异的学者，却不适合普通参观者，因为在他们看来所有展品都大同小异。

structure.text: much like storage rooms of objects packed together in showcases
good for scholars who wanted to study the subtle differences in design, but not for the ordinary visitor
who wanted to study the subtle differences in design
to whom it all looked alike

difficult_points: and some still do 是插入语，do 替代 look like storage rooms。
两个 who/whom 从句分别修饰 scholars 和 visitor；to whom 中介词提前。
冒号后省略了 they were，形成 good for ... but not for ... 的对比。

sentences.sentence: Similarly, the information accompanying the objects often made little sense to the lay visitor.
sentences.translation: 同样，展品附带的信息对普通参观者而言也常常难以理解。
difficult_points: accompanying ... 后置修饰 information。
make little sense to 表示对某人而言几乎无法理解；lay 指非专业的。

sentences.sentence: The content and format of explanations dated back to a time when the museum was the exclusive domain of the scientific researcher.
sentences.translation: 这些说明的内容和形式，可以追溯到博物馆还是科学研究人员专属领域的年代。

structure.text: The content and format of explanations
when the museum was the exclusive domain of the scientific researcher

difficult_points: content and format 是并列主语；date back to 表示起源可追溯至。
when 引导定语从句修饰 a time。

sentences.sentence: Recently, however, attitudes towards history and the way it should be presented have altered.
sentences.translation: 然而最近，人们对历史以及历史应如何呈现的态度已经改变。

structure.text: attitudes towards history and the way it should be presented

difficult_points: it should be presented 是省略 in which/that 的定语从句，修饰 the way，it 指 history。
have altered 使用现在完成时，强调态度变化影响至今。

sentences.sentence: The key word in heritage display is now 'experience', the more exciting the better and if possible, involving all the senses.
sentences.translation: 如今，遗产展示的关键词是“体验”——越刺激越好，而且如果可能，还要调动所有感官。

structure.text: The key word in heritage display

difficult_points: the more ... the better 是“越……越好”的比较结构。
if possible 省略 it is；involving ... 补充说明理想体验应具备的特征。

sentences.sentence: Good examples of this approach in the UK are the Jorvik Centre in York; the National Museum of Photography, Film and Television in Bradford; and the Imperial War Museum in London.
sentences.translation: 英国采用这种方式的典型例子包括约克的乔维克中心、布拉德福德的国家摄影电影电视博物馆，以及伦敦帝国战争博物馆。

structure.text: Good examples of this approach in the UK
the Jorvik Centre in York; the National Museum of Photography, Film and Television in Bradford; and the Imperial War Museum in London

difficult_points: 主语中心是 examples，因此系动词用 are。
分号将三个名称较长的并列例子清楚分隔。

sentences.sentence: In the US the trend emerged much earlier: Williamsburg has been a prototype for many heritage developments in other parts of the world.
sentences.translation: 在美国，这一趋势出现得早得多：威廉斯堡已成为世界其他地区许多遗产开发项目的原型。

structure.text: for many heritage developments in other parts of the world

difficult_points: 冒号后用威廉斯堡解释美国趋势较早的具体体现。
has been 强调其原型作用从过去持续至今。

sentences.sentence: No one can predict where the process will end.
sentences.translation: 没有人能够预测这一进程最终会走向何方。
difficult_points: where 从句作 predict 的宾语，使用陈述语序。
the process 指遗产展示日益重视沉浸体验的发展过程。

sentences.sentence: On so-called heritage sites the re-enactment of historical events is increasingly popular, and computers will soon provide virtual reality experiences, which will present visitors with a vivid image of the period of their choice, in which they themselves can act as if part of the historical environment.
sentences.translation: 在所谓的遗产景点，历史事件重演越来越受欢迎；计算机也将很快提供虚拟现实体验，向参观者呈现他们所选时代的生动画面，并让他们仿佛成为历史环境的一部分亲自参与其中。

structure.text: the re-enactment of historical events is increasingly popular
and computers will soon provide virtual reality experiences
which will present visitors with a vivid image of the period of their choice
in which they themselves can act as if part of the historical environment

difficult_points: which 修饰 virtual reality experiences；in which 修饰 the period，并把介词提前。
present visitors with ... 表示向参观者呈现；as if part of ... 省略 they were。
they themselves 强调参观者亲自参与。

sentences.sentence: Such developments have been criticized as an intolerable vulgarization, but the success of many historical theme parks and similar locations suggests that the majority of the public does not share this opinion.
sentences.translation: 这类发展受到批评，被认为是令人无法容忍的庸俗化；但许多历史主题公园及类似场所的成功表明，大多数公众并不赞同这种看法。

structure.text: Such developments have been criticized as an intolerable vulgarization
but the success of many historical theme parks and similar locations suggests
that the majority of the public does not share this opinion

difficult_points: be criticized as 表示被批评为某种事物。
that 从句作 suggests 的宾语；this opinion 指前面“不可容忍的庸俗化”这一批评。

sentences.sentence: In a related development, the sharp distinction between museum and heritage sites on the one hand, and theme parks on the other, is gradually evaporating.
sentences.translation: 在相关的发展中，博物馆及遗产景点与主题公园之间原本鲜明的界限正逐渐消失。

structure.text: between museum and heritage sites on the one hand, and theme parks on the other

difficult_points: between A and B 被 on the one hand/on the other 插入展开，比较两类场所。
evaporating 是比喻用法，表示界限正在逐渐消失。

expressions.text: on the one hand ... on the other

sentences.sentence: They already borrow ideas and concepts from one another.
sentences.translation: 它们已经开始彼此借鉴思想和概念。
difficult_points: They 指博物馆、遗产景点和主题公园。
one another 表示这些场所类型之间相互借鉴。

sentences.sentence: For example, museums have adopted story lines for exhibitions, sites have accepted 'theming' as a relevant tool, and theme parks are moving towards more authenticity and research-based presentations.
sentences.translation: 例如，博物馆开始为展览采用故事线，遗产景点接受“主题化”作为适用工具，而主题公园则朝着更真实、更以研究为基础的展示方向发展。

structure.text: museums have adopted story lines for exhibitions
sites have accepted 'theming' as a relevant tool
and theme parks are moving towards more authenticity and research-based presentations

difficult_points: 三个完整分句并列，分别说明三类场所彼此借鉴后的变化。
accept A as B 表示把主题化视为工具；research-based 是复合形容词。

sentences.sentence: In zoos, animals are no longer kept in cages, but in great spaces, either in the open air or in enormous greenhouses, such as the jungle and desert environments in Burgers' Zoo in Holland.
sentences.translation: 在动物园里，动物不再被关在笼中，而是生活在广阔空间里，这些空间可以位于户外，也可以设在巨大的温室中，例如荷兰伯格斯动物园营造的丛林和沙漠环境。

structure.text: animals are no longer kept in cages
either in the open air or in enormous greenhouses
such as the jungle and desert environments in Burgers' Zoo in Holland

difficult_points: but 后省略 are kept，构成 not in cages but in great spaces 的对比。
either ... or ... 说明 great spaces 的两种形式；such as 后举出具体环境。

sentences.sentence: This particular trend is regarded as one of the major developments in the presentation of natural history in the twentieth century.
sentences.translation: 这一具体趋势被视为二十世纪自然历史展示领域的重大发展之一。

structure.text: is regarded as one of the major developments
in the presentation of natural history

difficult_points: This particular trend 指动物园用大型自然化空间取代笼舍。
one of 后接复数 developments；be regarded as 表示被认为是。

sentences.sentence: Theme parks are undergoing other changes, too, as they try to present more serious social and cultural issues, and move away from fantasy.
sentences.translation: 主题公园也在经历其他变化，因为它们开始尝试呈现更严肃的社会文化议题，并逐渐远离幻想色彩。

structure.text: Theme parks are undergoing other changes
as they try to present more serious social and cultural issues

difficult_points: as 引导原因或伴随情形从句，解释变化的表现。
present 与 move 是共用主语 they 的并列动作；move away from 表示逐渐摆脱。

sentences.sentence: This development is a response to market forces and, although museums and heritage sites have a special, rather distinct, role to fulfil, they are also operating in a very competitive environment, where visitors make choices on how and where to spend their free time.
sentences.translation: 这种发展是对市场力量的回应；尽管博物馆和遗产景点肩负着特殊且相当独特的职责，它们也同样身处竞争激烈的环境，参观者会选择如何以及在哪里度过闲暇时间。

structure.text: This development is a response to market forces
although museums and heritage sites have a special, rather distinct, role to fulfil
they are also operating in a very competitive environment
where visitors make choices on how and where to spend their free time

difficult_points: although 从句让步承认博物馆使命特殊，但主句强调其仍面临竞争。
where 修饰 environment；how and where 并列引导 make choices on 的宾语内容。
to fulfil 后置修饰 role，表示需要履行的职责。

sentences.sentence: Heritage and museum experts do not have to invent stories and recreate historical environments to attract their visitors: their assets are already in place.
sentences.translation: 遗产和博物馆专家不必为了吸引参观者而编造故事、重建历史环境：他们所拥有的资源本来就已存在。

structure.text: do not have to invent stories and recreate historical environments
their assets are already in place

difficult_points: do not have to 表示“不必”，不是“禁止”。
冒号后的分句解释无需虚构的原因；in place 表示现成可用。

sentences.sentence: However, exhibits must be both based on artefacts and facts as we know them, and attractively presented.
sentences.translation: 然而，展览既必须以我们所掌握的文物与事实为基础，也必须以富有吸引力的方式呈现。

structure.text: must be both based on artefacts and facts as we know them

difficult_points: both ... and ... 连接两个被动要求，第二个 must be 被省略。
as we know them 修饰 artefacts and facts，强调依据现有认识呈现。

expressions.text: be based on artefacts and facts

sentences.sentence: Those who are professionally engaged in the art of interpreting history are thus in a difficult position, as they must steer a narrow course between the demands of 'evidence' and 'attractiveness', especially given the increasing need in the heritage industry for income-generating activities.
sentences.translation: 因此，专业从事历史阐释的人处境艰难，因为他们必须在“证据”与“吸引力”的要求之间谨慎寻找平衡，尤其是考虑到遗产行业越来越需要能够创收的活动。

structure.text: who are professionally engaged in the art of interpreting history
are thus in a difficult position
as they must steer a narrow course between the demands of 'evidence' and 'attractiveness'
given the increasing need in the heritage industry for income-generating activities

difficult_points: who 从句修饰 Those；as 从句解释他们处境困难的原因。
steer a narrow course 是比喻，指在两种相冲突的要求间谨慎平衡。
given 是介词“考虑到”，income-generating 表示能够创收的。

sentences.sentence: It could be claimed that in order to make everything in heritage more 'real', historical accuracy must be increasingly altered.
sentences.translation: 可以说，为了让遗产展示中的一切显得更“真实”，历史准确性反而必须被越来越多地改动。

structure.text: that in order to make everything in heritage more 'real', historical accuracy must be increasingly altered
in order to make everything in heritage more 'real'

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，that 从句是真正主语。
make + 宾语 + 形容词构成复合宾语；must be altered 是被动语态，形成“为真实而改史实”的悖论。

sentences.sentence: For example, Pithecanthropus erectus is depicted in an Indonesian museum with Malay facial features, because this corresponds to public perceptions.
sentences.translation: 例如，印度尼西亚一家博物馆把直立猿人塑造成具有马来人面部特征的形象，因为这样符合公众的认知。

structure.text: Pithecanthropus erectus is depicted in an Indonesian museum
because this corresponds to public perceptions

difficult_points: is depicted 使用被动语态，with 短语说明被塑造出的面部特征。
this 回指采用马来人面部特征的呈现方式。

sentences.sentence: Similarly, in the Museum of Natural History in Washington, Neanderthal man is shown making a dominant gesture to his wife.
sentences.translation: 同样，在华盛顿自然历史博物馆，尼安德特人被展示为正在对妻子做出支配性的手势。

structure.text: in the Museum of Natural History in Washington
making a dominant gesture to his wife

difficult_points: is shown making 构成被动感官/展示结构，making 描述被展示时的动作。
dominant gesture 带有现代人对性别权力关系的解释色彩。

sentences.sentence: Such presentations tell us more about contemporary perceptions of the world than about our ancestors.
sentences.translation: 这类展示告诉我们的，与其说是祖先的真实情况，不如说是当代人如何看待世界。

structure.text: more about contemporary perceptions of the world than about our ancestors

difficult_points: more about A than about B 表示展示反映 A 多于 B。
Such presentations 回指前两句带有现代文化特征的史前人类形象。

sentences.sentence: There is one compensation, however, for the professionals who make these interpretations: if they did not provide the interpretation, visitors would do it for themselves, based on their own ideas, misconceptions and prejudices.
sentences.translation: 不过，对作出这些阐释的专业人士而言，有一点可以聊作安慰：如果他们不提供解释，参观者就会依据自己的观念、误解和偏见自行解释。

structure.text: if they did not provide the interpretation
visitors would do it for themselves
based on their own ideas, misconceptions and prejudices

difficult_points: 冒号后是与现在事实相反的虚拟条件句：if + 过去式，主句 would do。
who 修饰 professionals；it 指 interpretation，for themselves 表示参观者自行完成。
based on ... 说明参观者解释历史的依据。

sentences.sentence: And no matter how exciting the result, it would contain a lot more bias than the presentations provided by experts.
sentences.translation: 而且，无论这种自行解释的结果多么精彩，它所包含的偏见都会比专家提供的展示多得多。

structure.text: no matter how exciting the result
would contain a lot more bias
than the presentations provided by experts

difficult_points: no matter how 引导让步从句，result 后省略 is。
it 指参观者自行作出的解释；provided by experts 修饰 presentations。

expressions.text: contain a lot more bias than

sentences.sentence: Human bias is inevitable, but another source of bias in the representation of history has to do with the transitory nature of the materials themselves.
sentences.translation: 人的偏见不可避免，但历史呈现中的另一种偏差来源，与材料本身短暂、易逝的性质有关。

structure.text: but another source of bias in the representation of history
has to do with the transitory nature of the materials themselves

difficult_points: but 对比人为解释偏见与材料保存偏差这两个来源。
have to do with 表示“与……有关”；themselves 强调材料本身的性质。

sentences.sentence: The simple fact is that not everything from history survives the historical process.
sentences.translation: 一个简单的事实是，并非历史上的一切都能在历史进程中保存下来。

structure.text: that not everything from history survives the historical process

difficult_points: that 从句作表语，说明 fact 的内容。
not everything 是部分否定，表示“并非所有事物”，而不是“一切都没有”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 经历历史过程而保存下来

sentences.sentence: Castles, palaces and cathedrals have a longer lifespan than the dwellings of ordinary people.
sentences.translation: 城堡、宫殿和大教堂的存续时间，比普通人的住所更长。

structure.text: than the dwellings of ordinary people

difficult_points: 三个名词并列作主语。
than 后比较的是两类建筑的 lifespan，省略 have。

sentences.sentence: The same applies to the furnishings and other contents of the premises.
sentences.translation: 同样的情况也适用于这些建筑中的家具和其他物品。

structure.text: applies to the furnishings and other contents of the premises

difficult_points: The same 回指富裕阶层建筑比普通住宅更容易留存的差异。
premises 指前述建筑及其内部空间，不是“前提”。

sentences.sentence: In a town like Leiden in Holland, which in the seventeenth century was occupied by approximately the same number of inhabitants as today, people lived within the walled town, an area more than five times smaller than modern Leiden.
sentences.translation: 以荷兰莱顿这样的城市为例，十七世纪时它的人口与今天大致相同，但当时人们居住在城墙围起的城区内，其面积比现代莱顿小五倍以上。

structure.text: In a town like Leiden in Holland
which in the seventeenth century was occupied by approximately the same number of inhabitants as today
people lived within the walled town
an area more than five times smaller than modern Leiden

difficult_points: which 修饰 Leiden，was occupied by 在此表示当时有多少居民居住。
an area ... 是 the walled town 的同位语；as today 后省略 the number of inhabitants is。
five times smaller 是原文表达，强调旧城区面积远小于现代城市。

sentences.sentence: In most of the houses several families lived together in circumstances beyond our imagination.
sentences.translation: 在大多数房屋中，几个家庭共同生活，其居住状况超乎我们的想象。
difficult_points: in circumstances ... 描述多个家庭共同居住的生活条件。
beyond our imagination 表示恶劣或拥挤程度超出现代人的想象。

expressions.meaning_zh: 在某种条件下共同生活

sentences.sentence: Yet in museums, fine period rooms give only an image of the lifestyle of the upper class of that era.
sentences.translation: 然而在博物馆里，精美的历史时期复原房间只呈现了那个时代上层阶级的生活方式。

structure.text: of the lifestyle of the upper class of that era

difficult_points: Yet 把普通人的拥挤现实与博物馆精美陈列形成对比。
period rooms 指按特定历史时期布置的复原房间；only 限制其代表性。

expressions.text: the upper class of that era

sentences.sentence: No wonder that people who stroll around exhibitions are filled with nostalgia; the evidence in museums indicates that life was so much better in the past.
sentences.translation: 难怪在展览中漫步的人会充满怀旧之情；博物馆中的证据似乎表明，过去的生活好得多。

structure.text: that people who stroll around exhibitions are filled with nostalgia
that life was so much better in the past

difficult_points: No wonder that ... 表示“难怪……”，who 从句修饰 people。
分号连接原因相近的两个完整分句；第二个 that 从句作 indicates 的宾语。
be filled with nostalgia 使用被动形式描述参观者的情绪状态。

sentences.sentence: This notion is induced by the bias in its representation in museums and heritage centers.
sentences.translation: 这种观念，是由博物馆和遗产中心在呈现历史时存在的偏差所造成的。

structure.text: by the bias in its representation in museums and heritage centers

difficult_points: This notion 回指“过去的生活好得多”这一印象。
its 指 history；is induced by 表示这种观念由呈现偏差引发。
